extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won a wind-shattered stage after late split at 10km with SKY trio ROWE-BERNAL-KWIATKOWSKI drilling the crosswinds to thin the group to seven riders
tactical_patterns: Sky deployed birthday boy ROWE plus BERNAL and KWIATKOWSKI to force late echelon split, sacrificing Bernal's GC position for team disruption.
Intermediate sprint at 25km positioned DQT riders GAVIRIA-DECLERCQ-NIZZOLO forward, yet all three still got dropped in final crosswind selection.
Final split occurred inside 10km after peloton regrouped, suggesting crosswind acceleration timing beats sustained early splitting on exposed roads.

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: Back-to-back wins including non-pure sprint finish shows bike-handling and positioning strength in chaos that pure fastmen lack
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: positive: Punctured mid-stage yet made front group of seven in final split, exceptional chase and wind-reading ability
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: SKY sacrificed his GC time (19 seconds back) to provide domestique work creating late split for team disruption
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: Lost 7 minutes on flat crosswind stage, fundamental weakness in echelon positioning for supposed GC contender
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: Finished 14+ minutes down on flat stage, catastrophic for third-place finisher from previous year who abandoned next day
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: neutral: European Champion made final seven-man group but launched sprint too early, tactical error not fitness deficit
